title: Pre-extraction Sample Processing for CALIBER Project
author: James JN Kitson

steps.title: Source steel beads (ball bearings) for...
steps.instruction: Source steel beads (ball bearings) for tissue grinding (Tungsten beads are not usually necessary). We use hardened carbon steel or stainless steel bearings from simplybearings.co.uk . This protocol requires two beads per sample tube.

steps.title: Beads are usually shipped coated in...
steps.instruction: Beads are usually shipped coated in manufacturing oil (especially the carbon steel beads). To remove this, place beads in a borosilicate glass beaker or Duran bottle with the pouring lip and lid removed then bake for at least 12 hours at 250 o C. Figure 1: Depending on baking time, carbon steel beads will change colour, this is normal.

steps.title: Prepare 5 ml screwcap collection tubes ( e
steps.instruction: Prepare 5 ml screwcap collection tubes ( e.g. Starlab #E1450-1100 ) containing three 3 mm hardened steel beads in batches of 96 tubes. Figure 2: Plant grinding tubes ready for plant material.

steps.title: Pick out a frozen plant sample...
steps.instruction: Pick out a frozen plant sample bag from the freezer and note the sample name. Figure 3: Typical sample bags with sample names highlighted.

steps.title: Write the sample name on a...
steps.instruction: Write the sample name on a manilla sample drying envelope and find the corresponding dried sample label. Figure 7: plant drying envelope and dried sample labels that are used to seal the flap.

steps.title: Using clean forceps, pick off all...
steps.instruction: Using clean forceps, pick off all insects and place in a screwcap tube of the appropriate size (2ml/5ml/15ml) then fill the tube with a solution of 95% Ethanol and 5% Glycerol.

steps.title: Store at -20 o C until...
steps.instruction: Store at -20 o C until transported to SASA for qPCR analysis. Figure 6: Box of insect tubes for longer term storage.

steps.title: Label the tube with the corresponding tube label. Figure 4
steps.instruction: Label the tube with the corresponding tube label. Figure 4: Insect tube labels. Figure 5: Labelled tube with insects ready for storage.

steps.title: Into this envelope, place approximately 2-5...
steps.instruction: Into this envelope, place approximately 2-5 g of leaf and chopped stem material.

steps.title: Seal with the dried sample label...
steps.instruction: Seal with the dried sample label and place in an oven overnight at 35-40 o C with a tray of silica gel in the bottom. Figure 8: Plant sample drying over silica in the oven.

steps.title: The archive sample is ready for...
steps.instruction: The archive sample is ready for long term storage at room temperature.

steps.title: Using a clean scalpel, slice and...
steps.instruction: Using a clean scalpel, slice and place ~ 50 mg of plant material into the pre-prepared collection tubes containing the hardened steel ball bearings (from step 3).

steps.title: Label the tube with the corresponding tube label. Figure 9
steps.instruction: Label the tube with the corresponding tube label. Figure 9: Plant grinding tube with label, sample and ball bearings ready for storage.

steps.title: Store at -20 o C until...
steps.instruction: Store at -20 o C until ready to proceed with DNA extraction.

steps.title: The remaining plant material and packaging...
steps.instruction: The remaining plant material and packaging can be disposed of in a clinical waste bin.

steps.title: Wash forceps and scalpel in 1x chemgene
steps.instruction: Wash forceps and scalpel in 1x chemgene.

steps.title: Rinse forceps and scalpel in molecular grade water
steps.instruction: Rinse forceps and scalpel in molecular grade water.

steps.title: Rinse forceps in 100% Isopropanol and leave to dry
steps.instruction: Rinse forceps in 100% Isopropanol and leave to dry.

steps.title: Wipe down dissection area with blue roll and 1x chemgene
steps.instruction: Wipe down dissection area with blue roll and 1x chemgene.
